1→defmodule TuistCommon.SentryEventFilter do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Shared Sentry event filter logic for Tuist services.
     4→
     5→  This module provides a `before_send` callback that filters out expected errors
     6→  that are not actionable. Services can use this directly or extend it with
     7→  additional exceptions.
     8→
     9→  ## Usage
    10→
    11→  In your config (with default exceptions only):
    12→
    13→      config :sentry,
    14→        before_send: {TuistCommon.SentryEventFilter, :before_send}
    15→
    16→  With additional exceptions:
    17→
    18→      config :sentry,
    19→        before_send: {TuistCommon.SentryEventFilter, :before_send, [[MyApp.CustomError]]}
    20→  """
    21→
    22→  @default_ignored_exceptions [
    23→    Bandit.TransportError,
    24→    Phoenix.Router.NoRouteError
    25→  ]
    26→
    27→  @doc """
    28→  Filters Sentry events, returning `false` for ignored exceptions or the event otherwise.
    29→
    30→  Can be used directly as a `before_send` callback or called with additional exceptions.
    31→  """
    32→  def before_send(event, additional_ignored \\ [])
    33→
    34→  def before_send(
    35→        %Sentry.Event{original_exception: exception, source: event_source} = event,
    36→        additional_ignored
    37→      )
    38→      when is_exception(exception) do
    39→    ignored = ignored_exceptions(additional_ignored)
    40→
    41→    if exception.__struct__ in ignored or
    42→         Sentry.DefaultEventFilter.exclude_exception?(exception, event_source) do
    43→      false
    44→    else
    45→      event
    46→    end
    47→  end
    48→
    49→  def before_send(%Sentry.Event{} = event, additional_ignored) do
    50→    ignored = ignored_exceptions(additional_ignored)
    51→
    52→    if ignored_exception_type?(event, ignored) do
    53→      false
    54→    else
    55→      event
    56→    end
    57→  end
    58→
    59→  def before_send(event, _additional_ignored), do: event
    60→
    61→  defp ignored_exceptions(additional_ignored) do
    62→    @default_ignored_exceptions ++ additional_ignored
    63→  end
    64→
    65→  defp ignored_exception_type?(%Sentry.Event{exception: exceptions}, ignored)
    66→       when is_list(exceptions) do
    67→    ignored_types =
    68→      ignored
    69→      |> Enum.map(&inspect/1)
    70→      |> MapSet.new()
    71→
    72→    Enum.any?(exceptions, fn
    73→      %Sentry.Interfaces.Exception{type: type} -> MapSet.member?(ignored_types, type)
    74→      %{type: type} when is_binary(type) -> MapSet.member?(ignored_types, type)
    75→      _ -> false
    76→    end)
    77→  end
    78→
    79→  defp ignored_exception_type?(_event, _ignored), do: false
    80→end
    81→

1→defmodule TuistWeb.Plugs.SentryContextPlug do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  A plug that sets Sentry context data based on the authenticated subject
     4→  and selected resources. Only IDs are used to keep data anonymous.
     5→  """
     6→
     7→  alias Tuist.Accounts.AuthenticatedAccount
     8→  alias Tuist.Accounts.User
     9→  alias Tuist.Projects.Project
    10→
    11→  def init(opts), do: opts
    12→
    13→  def call(conn, _opts) do
    14→    if Tuist.Environment.error_tracking_enabled?() do
    15→      auth_data = get_auth_data(conn)
    16→      set_context(auth_data)
    17→    end
    18→
    19→    conn
    20→  end
    21→
    22→  @doc """
    23→  Sets Sentry context for the selected project and/or account.
    24→  Call this after assigning :selected_project or :selected_account to the conn.
    25→  """
    26→  def set_selection_context(conn) do
    27→    if Tuist.Environment.error_tracking_enabled?() do
    28→      selection_data = get_selection_data(conn)
    29→      set_context(selection_data)
    30→    end
    31→
    32→    conn
    33→  end
    34→
    35→  defp get_auth_data(conn) do
    36→    case TuistWeb.Authentication.authenticated_subject(conn) do
    37→      %User{id: user_id, account: %{id: account_id, name: account_handle}} ->
    38→        %{
    39→          auth_user_id: user_id,
    40→          auth_account_id: account_id,
    41→          auth_account_handle: account_handle
    42→        }
    43→
    44→      %Project{id: project_id, account: %{id: account_id, name: account_handle}} ->
    45→        %{
    46→          auth_project_id: project_id,
    47→          auth_account_id: account_id,
    48→          auth_account_handle: account_handle
    49→        }
    50→
    51→      %AuthenticatedAccount{account: %{id: account_id, name: account_handle}} ->
    52→        %{auth_account_id: account_id, auth_account_handle: account_handle}
    53→
    54→      nil ->
    55→        %{}
    56→    end
    57→  end
    58→
    59→  defp get_selection_data(conn) do
    60→    case {conn.assigns[:selected_project], conn.assigns[:selected_account]} do
    61→      {%{id: project_id, name: project_handle}, %{id: account_id, name: account_handle, customer_id: customer_id}} ->
    62→        maybe_put(
    63→          %{
    64→            selected_project_id: project_id,
    65→            selected_project_handle: project_handle,
    66→            selected_account_id: account_id,
    67→            selected_account_handle: account_handle
    68→          },
    69→          :selected_account_customer_id,
    70→          customer_id
    71→        )
    72→
    73→      {_, %{id: account_id, name: account_handle, customer_id: customer_id}} ->
    74→        maybe_put(
    75→          %{selected_account_id: account_id, selected_account_handle: account_handle},
    76→          :selected_account_customer_id,
    77→          customer_id
    78→        )
    79→
    80→      _ ->
    81→        %{}
    82→    end
    83→  end
    84→
    85→  defp set_context(context) when context == %{} do
    86→    :ok
    87→  end
    88→
    89→  defp set_context(context) do
    90→    Sentry.Context.set_extra_context(context)
    91→  end
    92→
    93→  defp maybe_put(map, _key, nil), do: map
    94→  defp maybe_put(map, key, value), do: Map.put(map, key, value)
    95→end
    96→

1→import Config
     2→
     3→if config_env() == :prod do
     4→  secret_key_base =
     5→    System.get_env("SECRET_KEY_BASE") ||
     6→      raise """
     7→      environment variable SECRET_KEY_BASE is missing.
     8→      You can generate one by calling: mix phx.gen.secret
     9→      """
    10→
    11→  host = System.get_env("PUBLIC_HOST")
    12→  port = String.to_integer(System.get_env("PORT") || "4000")
    13→  socket_path = System.get_env("PHX_SOCKET_PATH")
    14→
    15→  http_config =
    16→    case socket_path do
    17→      nil ->
    18→        [
    19→          ip: {0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0},
    20→          port: port
    21→        ]
    22→
    23→      path ->
    24→        File.mkdir_p!(Path.dirname(path))
    25→        _ = File.rm(path)
    26→
    27→        [
    28→          ip: {:local, path},
    29→          port: 0
    30→        ]
    31→    end
    32→
    33→  sentry_dsn = System.get_env("SENTRY_DSN_CACHE")
    34→
    35→  if sentry_dsn do
    36→    config :sentry,
    37→      dsn: sentry_dsn,
    38→      environment_name: System.get_env("SENTRY_ENV") || "production"
    39→  end
    40→
    41→  s3_endpoint = System.get_env("S3_ENDPOINT")
    42→
    43→  {s3_scheme, s3_host} =
    44→    case s3_endpoint do
    45→      nil ->
    46→        {"https://", System.get_env("S3_HOST")}
    47→
    48→      "" ->
    49→        {"https://", System.get_env("S3_HOST")}
    50→
    51→      endpoint ->
    52→        uri = URI.parse(endpoint)
    53→
    54→        host =
    55→          case {uri.host, uri.port} do
    56→            {nil, _} -> System.get_env("S3_HOST")
    57→            {host, nil} -> host
    58→            {host, port} -> "#{host}:#{port}"
    59→          end
    60→
    61→        scheme = (uri.scheme || "https") <> "://"
    62→        {scheme, host}
    63→    end
    64→
    65→  s3_access_key_id = System.get_env("S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID") || System.get_env("AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID")
    66→  s3_secret_access_key = System.get_env("S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY") || System.get_env("AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY")
    67→  s3_region = System.get_env("S3_REGION") || System.get_env("AWS_REGION")
    68→
    69→  otel_endpoint = System.get_env("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT")
    70→
    71→  config :cache, Cache.Guardian,
    72→    issuer: "tuist",
    73→    secret_key: System.get_env("GUARDIAN_SECRET_KEY")
    74→
    75→  config :cache, Cache.Repo,
    76→    database: "/data/repo.sqlite",

1→defmodule TuistWeb.Endpoint do
     2→  use Sentry.PlugCapture
     3→  use Phoenix.Endpoint, otp_app: :tuist
     4→
     5→  # The session will be stored in the cookie and signed,
     6→  # this means its contents can be read but not tampered with.
     7→  # Set :encryption_salt if you would also like to encrypt it.
     8→  alias TuistWeb.Plugs.WebhookPlug
     9→  alias TuistWeb.Webhooks.BillingController
    10→  alias TuistWeb.Webhooks.GitHubController
    11→
    12→  @session_options [
    13→    store: :cookie,
    14→    key: "_tuist_key",
    15→    signing_salt: "tmgjS63H",
    16→    same_site: "Lax"
    17→  ]
    18→
    19→  socket "/live", Phoenix.LiveView.Socket,
    20→    websocket: [connect_info: [:user_agent, session: @session_options]],
    21→    longpoll: [connect_info: [session: @session_options]]
    22→
    23→  socket "/socket", TuistWeb.Socket,
    24→    websocket: true,
    25→    longpoll: false
    26→
    27→  # Serve at "/" the static files from "priv/static" directory.
    28→  #
    29→  # You should set gzip to true if you are running phx.digest
    30→  # when deploying your static files in production.
    31→  plug Plug.Static,
    32→    at: "/",
    33→    from: :tuist,
    34→    gzip: true,
    35→    cache_control_for_etags: "public, max-age=31536000, immutable",
    36→    only: TuistWeb.static_paths()
    37→
    38→  if Code.ensure_loaded?(Tidewave) do
    39→    plug Tidewave
    40→  end
    41→
    42→  # Code reloading can be explicitly enabled under the
    43→  # :code_reloader configuration of your endpoint.
    44→  if code_reloading? do
    45→    socket "/phoenix/live_reload/socket", Phoenix.LiveReloader.Socket
    46→    plug Phoenix.LiveReloader
    47→    plug Phoenix.CodeReloader
    48→    plug Phoenix.Ecto.CheckRepoStatus, otp_app: :tuist
    49→  end
    50→
    51→  plug Phoenix.LiveDashboard.RequestLogger,
    52→    param_key: "request_logger",
    53→    cookie_key: "request_logger"
    54→
    55→  plug Plug.RequestId
    56→  plug TuistCommon.OtelRequestIdPlug
    57→  plug Plug.Telemetry, event_prefix: [:phoenix, :endpoint]
    58→  plug Sentry.PlugContext
    59→  plug TuistWeb.Plugs.CloseConnectionOnErrorPlug
    60→
    61→  plug Stripe.WebhookPlug,
    62→    at: "/webhooks/stripe",
    63→    handler: BillingController,
    64→    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :stripe_endpoint_secret, []}
    65→
    66→  plug WebhookPlug,
    67→    at: "/webhooks/github",
    68→    handler: GitHubController,
    69→    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :github_app_webhook_secret, []},
    70→    signature_header: "x-hub-signature-256",
    71→    signature_prefix: "sha256=",
    72→    read_timeout: 60_000
    73→
    74→  plug WebhookPlug,
    75→    at: "/webhooks/cache",
    76→    handler: TuistWeb.Webhooks.CacheController,
    77→    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :cache_api_key, []},
    78→    signature_header: "x-cache-signature"
    79→
    80→  plug WebhookPlug,
    81→    at: "/webhooks/gradle-cache",
    82→    handler: TuistWeb.Webhooks.GradleCacheController,
    83→    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :cache_api_key, []},
    84→    signature_header: "x-cache-signature"
    85→
    86→  # The /api/runs endpoint can receive large payloads (files, cacheable_tasks, cas_outputs)
    87→  # for projects with thousands of files. 50MB should accommodate most projects.
    88→  # TODO: Consider streaming large arrays instead of loading everything into memory.
    89→  plug Plug.Parsers,
    90→    parsers: [:urlencoded, :multipart, :json],
    91→    pass: ["*/*"],
    92→    json_decoder: Phoenix.json_library(),
    93→    length: 50_000_000
    94→
    95→  plug Plug.MethodOverride
    96→  plug Plug.Head
    97→  plug Plug.Session, @session_options
    98→  plug TuistWeb.Router
    99→end
   100→

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings:

## Sentry Configuration Summary

I've found comprehensive Sentry configuration in both the server and cache services. Here's a detailed report:

### SERVER (Elixir/Phoenix in `server/`)

**1. Configuration Files:**
- **Runtime Configuration** (`server/config/runtime.exs`, lines 218-226):
  ```elixir
  if Tuist.Environment.error_tracking_enabled?() do
    config :sentry,
      client: TuistCommon.SentryHTTPClient,
      dsn: Tuist.Environment.sentry_dsn(secrets),
      environment_name: env,
      enable_source_code_context: true,
      root_source_code_paths: [File.cwd!()],
      before_send: {Tuist.SentryEventFilter, :before_send}
  end
  ```
- **Test Configuration** (`server/config/test.exs`, line 13):
  ```elixir
  config :sentry, dsn: nil
  ```

**2. Sentry Event Filter** (`server/lib/tuist/sentry_event_filter.ex`):
  - This is a wrapper module that delegates to `TuistCommon.SentryEventFilter`
  - Adds server-specific ignored exceptions:
    - `TuistWeb.Errors.BadRequestError`
    - `TuistWeb.Errors.NotFoundError`
    - `TuistWeb.Errors.TooManyRequestsError`
    - `TuistWeb.Errors.UnauthorizedError`

**3. Sentry Context Plug** (`server/lib/tuist_web/plugs/sentry_context_plug.ex`):
  - Sets Sentry context based on authenticated user/project/account
  - Uses IDs only for anonymity (no sensitive data)
  - Provides two methods:
    - `call/2` - Sets context from authentication data in connection
    - `set_selection_context/1` - Sets context from selected project/account

**4. Endpoint Integration** (`server/lib/tuist_web/endpoint.ex`):
  - Uses `Sentry.PlugCapture` module hook
  - Includes `Sentry.PlugContext` plug (line 58)

**5. Environment Control** (`server/lib/tuist/environment.ex`):
  - `error_tracking_enabled?()` - Enables Sentry only when:
    - `TUIST_FORCE_ERROR_TRACKING` is truthy, OR
    - Running on Tuist-hosted instance AND in `:prod`, `:stag`, or `:can` environments
  - `sentry_dsn(secrets)` - Retrieves DSN from encrypted secrets under `:sentry, :dsn` path

---

### CACHE (Elixir/Phoenix in `cache/`)

**1. Configuration Files:**
- **Base Configuration** (`cache/config/config.exs`, lines 82-86):
  ```elixir
  config :sentry,
    client: TuistCommon.SentryHTTPClient,
    enable_source_code_context: true,
    root_source_code_paths: [File.cwd!()],
    before_send: {TuistCommon.SentryEventFilter, :before_send}
  ```
- **Runtime Configuration** (`cache/config/runtime.exs`, lines 33-39):
  ```elixir
  sentry_dsn = System.get_env("SENTRY_DSN_CACHE")
  
  if sentry_dsn do
    config :sentry,
      dsn: sentry_dsn,
      environment_name: System.get_env("SENTRY_ENV") || "production"
  ```

file.content:
defmodule TuistCommon.SentryEventFilter do
  @moduledoc """
  Shared Sentry event filter logic for Tuist services.

  This module provides a `before_send` callback that filters out expected errors
  that are not actionable. Services can use this directly or extend it with
  additional exceptions.

  ## Usage

  In your config (with default exceptions only):

      config :sentry,
        before_send: {TuistCommon.SentryEventFilter, :before_send}

  With additional exceptions:

      config :sentry,
        before_send: {TuistCommon.SentryEventFilter, :before_send, [[MyApp.CustomError]]}
  """

  @default_ignored_exceptions [
    Bandit.TransportError,
    Phoenix.Router.NoRouteError
  ]

  @doc """
  Filters Sentry events, returning `false` for ignored exceptions or the event otherwise.

  Can be used directly as a `before_send` callback or called with additional exceptions.
  """
  def before_send(event, additional_ignored \\ [])

  def before_send(
        %Sentry.Event{original_exception: exception, source: event_source} = event,
        additional_ignored
      )
      when is_exception(exception) do
    ignored = ignored_exceptions(additional_ignored)

    if exception.__struct__ in ignored or
         Sentry.DefaultEventFilter.exclude_exception?(exception, event_source) do
      false
    else
      event
    end
  end

  def before_send(%Sentry.Event{} = event, additional_ignored) do
    ignored = ignored_exceptions(additional_ignored)

    if ignored_exception_type?(event, ignored) do
      false
    else
      event
    end
  end

  def before_send(event, _additional_ignored), do: event

  defp ignored_exceptions(additional_ignored) do
    @default_ignored_exceptions ++ additional_ignored
  end

  defp ignored_exception_type?(%Sentry.Event{exception: exceptions}, ignored)
       when is_list(exceptions) do
    ignored_types =
      ignored
      |> Enum.map(&inspect/1)
      |> MapSet.new()

    Enum.any?(exceptions, fn
      %Sentry.Interfaces.Exception{type: type} -> MapSet.member?(ignored_types, type)
      %{type: type} when is_binary(type) -> MapSet.member?(ignored_types, type)
      _ -> false
    end)
  end

  defp ignored_exception_type?(_event, _ignored), do: false
end

content.text: The fix is simple -- just add `Bandit.HTTPError` to the `@default_ignored_exceptions` list. This will filter it out in both the server (GitHub webhook timeouts) and the cache (body read timeouts during CAS uploads).

defmodule TuistCommon.SentryEventFilterTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  alias TuistCommon.SentryEventFilter

  defp event(overrides) do
    defaults = %{
      event_id: String.duplicate("a", 32),
      timestamp: "2020-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    }

    struct!(Sentry.Event, Map.merge(defaults, overrides))
  end

  describe "before_send/1" do
    test "excludes Bandit.TransportError" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %Bandit.TransportError{message: "test"}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == false
    end

    test "excludes Phoenix.Router.NoRouteError" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %Phoenix.Router.NoRouteError{}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == false
    end

    test "allows other exceptions through" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %RuntimeError{message: "test"}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == event
    end

    test "allows events without original_exception through" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: nil, exception: []})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == event
    end

    test "excludes ignored exception types even without original_exception" do
      event =
        event(%{
          original_exception: nil,
          exception: [
            %Sentry.Interfaces.Exception{type: "Phoenix.Router.NoRouteError", value: "no route"}
          ]
        })

      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == false
    end
  end

  describe "before_send/2 with additional exceptions" do
    test "excludes additional exceptions" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %ArgumentError{message: "test"}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event, [ArgumentError]) == false
    end

    test "still excludes default exceptions" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %Bandit.TransportError{message: "test"}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event, [ArgumentError]) == false
    end

    test "allows non-listed exceptions through" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %RuntimeError{message: "test"}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event, [ArgumentError]) == event
    end
  end
end

      timestamp: "2020-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    }

    struct!(Sentry.Event, Map.merge(defaults, overrides))
  end

  describe "before_send/1" do
    test "excludes Bandit.TransportError" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %Bandit.TransportError{message: "test"}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == false
    end

    test "excludes Phoenix.Router.NoRouteError" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %Phoenix.Router.NoRouteError{}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == false
    end

    test "allows other exceptions through" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %RuntimeError{message: "test"}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == event
    end

    test "allows events without original_exception through" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: nil, exception: []})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == event
    end

    test "excludes ignored exception types even without original_exception" do
      event =
        event(%{
          original_exception: nil,
          exception: [
            %Sentry.Interfaces.Exception{type: "Phoenix.Router.NoRouteError", value: "no route"}
          ]
        })

      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == false
    end
  end

  describe "before_send/2 with additional exceptions" do
    test "excludes additional exceptions" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %ArgumentError{message: "test"}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event, [ArgumentError]) == false
    end

    test "still excludes default exceptions" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %Bandit.TransportError{message: "test"}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event, [ArgumentError]) == false
    end

    test "allows non-listed exceptions through" do
      event = event(%{original_exception: %RuntimeError{message: "test"}})
      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event, [ArgumentError]) == event
    end
  end
end

content.text:
All 9 tests pass. Here's a summary of the change:

**Problem:** `Bandit.HTTPError: Body read timeout` was being reported to Sentry ~55K times total across both the server (TUIST-7, 29K events on `/webhooks/github`) and cache (CACHE-7, 25K events on CAS uploads). These are expected errors when clients disconnect or are slow sending request bodies.

**Fix:** Added `Bandit.HTTPError` to the `@default_ignored_exceptions` list in `tuist_common/lib/tuist_common/sentry_event_filter.ex`. Since both the server and cache use this shared filter via their `before_send` config, this single change filters the error in both projects.

Would you like me to commit this?

message.content: I want you to create a PR with the fix. What about the issue in the case of the cache server?

content.text: Let me re-read the cache issue -- the stacktrace shows it goes through `Cache.BodyReader.drain/1`. Let me check that code and understand if there's a better way to handle it there too.

content.content:
1→defmodule Cache.BodyReader do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Handles reading request bodies from Plug connections.
     4→
     5→  Small bodies are read into memory, large bodies are streamed to temporary files
     6→  to avoid memory pressure.
     7→
     8→  ## Timeout Strategy
     9→
    10→  Uses progressive timeouts inspired by Apache's `mod_reqtimeout` MinRate:
    11→  - Initial timeout is calculated based on Content-Length and minimum throughput
    12→  - Each subsequent chunk read uses a fresh timeout based on chunk size
    13→  - This allows slow-but-steady connections while timing out stalled transfers
    14→
    15→  See `TuistCommon.BodyReader` for the timeout calculation logic.
    16→  """
    17→
    18→  @max_upload_bytes 25 * 1024 * 1024
    19→  @default_opts [length: @max_upload_bytes, read_length: 262_144]
    20→
    21→  @doc """
    22→  Reads the request body from the connection.
    23→
    24→  Returns:
    25→  - `{:ok, body, conn}` - For small bodies that fit in memory
    26→  - `{:ok, {:file, tmp_path}, conn}` - For large bodies streamed to temp file
    27→  - `{:error, reason, conn}` - For errors like :too_large, :timeout, etc.
    28→  """
    29→
    30→  def read(conn, opts \\ []) do
    31→    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(read_opts(conn), opts)
    32→    max_bytes = Keyword.get(opts, :max_bytes, @max_upload_bytes)
    33→
    34→    conn
    35→    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
    36→    |> handle_read_result(conn, merged_opts, :store, max_bytes)
    37→  rescue
    38→    Bandit.TransportError ->
    39→      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
    40→  end
    41→
    42→  @doc """
    43→  Reads the request body and writes it directly to an IO device.
    44→
    45→  Returns `{:ok, bytes_written, conn}` on success,
    46→  or `{:error, reason, conn}` on failure.
    47→  """
    48→  def read_to_device(conn, device, opts \\ []) do
    49→    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(read_opts(conn), opts)
    50→    max_bytes = Keyword.get(opts, :max_bytes, @max_upload_bytes)
    51→    writer = fn chunk -> :file.write(device, chunk) end
    52→
    53→    conn
    54→    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
    55→    |> handle_device_result(conn, merged_opts, writer, max_bytes)
    56→  rescue
    57→    Bandit.TransportError ->
    58→      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
    59→  end
    60→
    61→  defp handle_device_result({:ok, body, conn_after}, _conn, _opts, writer, max_bytes) do
    62→    bytes = byte_size(body)
    63→
    64→    cond do
    65→      bytes > max_bytes -> {:error, :too_large, conn_after}
    66→      bytes == 0 -> {:ok, 0, conn_after}
    67→      true -> write_device_body(writer, body, bytes, conn_after)
    68→    end
    69→  end
    70→
    71→  defp handle_device_result({:more, chunk, conn_after}, _conn, opts, writer, max_bytes) do
    72→    bytes_read = byte_size(chunk)
    73→
    74→    if bytes_read > max_bytes do
    75→      {:error, :too_large, conn_after}
    76→    else
    77→      with :ok <- writer.(chunk),
    78→           {:ok, conn_final, total_bytes} <- read_loop(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
    79→        {:ok, total_bytes, conn_final}
    80→      else
    81→        {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason, conn_after}
    82→        {:error, reason, conn_final} -> {:error, reason, conn_final}
    83→      end
    84→    end
    85→  end
    86→

defmodule Cache.BodyReader do
  @moduledoc """
  Handles reading request bodies from Plug connections.

  Small bodies are read into memory, large bodies are streamed to temporary files
  to avoid memory pressure.

  ## Timeout Strategy

  Uses progressive timeouts inspired by Apache's `mod_reqtimeout` MinRate:
  - Initial timeout is calculated based on Content-Length and minimum throughput
  - Each subsequent chunk read uses a fresh timeout based on chunk size
  - This allows slow-but-steady connections while timing out stalled transfers

  See `TuistCommon.BodyReader` for the timeout calculation logic.
  """

  @max_upload_bytes 25 * 1024 * 1024
  @default_opts [length: @max_upload_bytes, read_length: 262_144]

  @doc """
  Reads the request body from the connection.

  Returns:
  - `{:ok, body, conn}` - For small bodies that fit in memory
  - `{:ok, {:file, tmp_path}, conn}` - For large bodies streamed to temp file
  - `{:error, reason, conn}` - For errors like :too_large, :timeout, etc.
  """

  def read(conn, opts \\ []) do
    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(read_opts(conn), opts)
    max_bytes = Keyword.get(opts, :max_bytes, @max_upload_bytes)

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
    |> handle_read_result(conn, merged_opts, :store, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  @doc """
  Reads the request body and writes it directly to an IO device.

  Returns `{:ok, bytes_written, conn}` on success,
  or `{:error, reason, conn}` on failure.
  """
  def read_to_device(conn, device, opts \\ []) do
    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(read_opts(conn), opts)
    max_bytes = Keyword.get(opts, :max_bytes, @max_upload_bytes)
    writer = fn chunk -> :file.write(device, chunk) end

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
    |> handle_device_result(conn, merged_opts, writer, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:ok, body, conn_after}, _conn, _opts, writer, max_bytes) do
    bytes = byte_size(body)

    cond do
      bytes > max_bytes -> {:error, :too_large, conn_after}
      bytes == 0 -> {:ok, 0, conn_after}
      true -> write_device_body(writer, body, bytes, conn_after)
    end
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:more, chunk, conn_after}, _conn, opts, writer, max_bytes) do
    bytes_read = byte_size(chunk)

    if bytes_read > max_bytes do
      {:error, :too_large, conn_after}
    else
      with :ok <- writer.(chunk),
           {:ok, conn_final, total_bytes} <- read_loop(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
        {:ok, total_bytes, conn_final}
      else
        {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason, conn_after}
        {:error, reason, conn_final} -> {:error, reason, conn_final}
      end
    end
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:error, reason}, conn, _opts, _writer, _max_bytes) do
    normalize_error(reason, conn)
  end

  defp write_device_body(writer, body, bytes, conn_after) do
    case writer.(body) do
      :ok -> {:ok, bytes, conn_after}
      {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason, conn_after}
    end
  end

  @doc """
  Drains the request body without storing it.
  Useful when the upload already exists and we need to consume the body.
  """

  def drain(conn) do
    opts = read_opts(conn)

    case conn |> Plug.Conn.read_body(opts) |> handle_read_result(conn, opts, :discard, @max_upload_bytes) do
      {:ok, _, conn_after} -> {:ok, conn_after}
      {:error, _reason, conn_after} -> {:error, conn_after}
    end
  end

  defp handle_read_result(result, conn, opts, mode, max_bytes) do
    case result do
      {:ok, body, conn_after} ->
        {:ok, body, conn_after}

      {:more, chunk, conn_after} ->
        read_chunks(conn_after, opts, chunk, byte_size(chunk), mode, max_bytes)

      {:error, :too_large} ->
        {:error, :too_large, conn}

      {:error, reason} when reason in [:timeout, :econnaborted] ->
        {:error, :timeout, conn}

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp normalize_error(:too_large, conn), do: {:error, :too_large, conn}
  defp normalize_error(reason, conn) when reason in [:timeout, :econnaborted], do: {:error, :timeout, conn}
  defp normalize_error(reason, conn), do: {:error, reason, conn}

  defp read_chunks(conn, opts, _first_chunk, bytes_read, :discard, max_bytes) do
    read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, fn _chunk -> :ok end, max_bytes)
  end

  defp read_chunks(conn, opts, first_chunk, bytes_read, :store, max_bytes) do
    tmp_path = tmp_path(opts)

    case File.open(tmp_path, [:write, :binary]) do
      {:ok, device} ->
        writer = fn chunk -> :file.write(device, chunk) end

        case writer.(first_chunk) do
          :ok ->
            case read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
              {:ok, conn_after, _bytes} ->
                File.close(device)
                {:ok, {:file, tmp_path}, conn_after}

              {:error, reason, conn_after} ->
                File.close(device)
                File.rm(tmp_path)
                {:error, reason, conn_after}
            end

          {:error, reason} ->
            File.close(device)
            File.rm(tmp_path)
            {:error, reason, conn}
        end

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
    chunk_opts = Keyword.put(opts, :read_timeout, chunk_timeout(opts))

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(chunk_opts)
    |> handle_loop_result(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  defp handle_loop_result(result, conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
    case result do
      {:ok, "", conn_after} ->
        {:ok, conn_after, bytes_read}

      {:ok, chunk, conn_after} ->
        write_chunk(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, false)

      {:more, chunk, conn_after} ->
        write_chunk(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, true)

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp write_chunk(conn, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, recurse?) do
    bytes = bytes_read + byte_size(chunk)

    if bytes > max_bytes do
      {:error, :too_large, conn}
    else
      case writer.(chunk) do
        :ok ->
          if recurse? do
            read_loop(conn, opts, bytes, writer, max_bytes)
          else
            {:ok, conn, bytes}
          end

        {:error, reason} ->
          {:error, reason, conn}
      end
    end
  end

  defp tmp_path(opts) do
    base = Keyword.get(opts, :tmp_dir, System.tmp_dir!())
    unique = :erlang.unique_integer([:positive, :monotonic])
    Path.join(base, ".cache-upload-#{unique}")
  end

  defp read_opts(conn) do
    base_opts = Map.get(conn.private, :body_read_opts, @default_opts)
    TuistCommon.BodyReader.read_opts(conn, base_opts)
  end

  defp chunk_timeout(opts) do
    TuistCommon.BodyReader.chunk_timeout(opts)
  end
end

1→defmodule CacheWeb.CASController do
     2→  use CacheWeb, :controller
     3→  use OpenApiSpex.ControllerSpecs
     4→
     5→  alias Cache.BodyReader
     6→  alias Cache.CacheArtifacts
     7→  alias Cache.CAS
     8→  alias Cache.S3
     9→  alias Cache.S3Transfers
    10→  alias CacheWeb.API.Schemas.Error
    11→
    12→  require Logger
    13→
    14→  plug OpenApiSpex.Plug.CastAndValidate,
    15→    json_render_error_v2: true
    16→
    17→  tags(["CAS"])
    18→
    19→  operation(:download,
    20→    summary: "Download a CAS artifact",
    21→    operation_id: "downloadCASArtifact",
    22→    parameters: [
    23→      id: [
    24→        in: :path,
    25→        type: :string,
    26→        required: true,
    27→        description: "The artifact identifier"
    28→      ],
    29→      account_handle: [
    30→        in: :query,
    31→        type: :string,
    32→        required: true,
    33→        description: "The handle of the account"
    34→      ],
    35→      project_handle: [
    36→        in: :query,
    37→        type: :string,
    38→        required: true,
    39→        description: "The handle of the project"
    40→      ]
    41→    ],
    42→    responses: %{
    43→      ok: {"Artifact content", "application/octet-stream", nil},
    44→      not_found: {"Artifact not found", "application/json", Error},
    45→      unauthorized: {"Unauthorized", "application/json", Error},
    46→      forbidden: {"Forbidden", "application/json", Error},
    47→      bad_request: {"Bad request", "application/json", Error}
    48→    }
    49→  )
    50→
    51→  def download(conn, %{id: id, account_handle: account_handle, project_handle: project_handle}) do
    52→    :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :download, :hit], %{}, %{})
    53→    key = CAS.Disk.key(account_handle, project_handle, id)
    54→    :ok = CacheArtifacts.track_artifact_access(key)
    55→
    56→    case CAS.Disk.stat(account_handle, project_handle, id) do
    57→      {:ok, %File.Stat{size: size}} ->
    58→        local_path = CAS.Disk.local_accel_path(account_handle, project_handle, id)
    59→
    60→        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :download, :disk_hit], %{size: size}, %{
    61→          cas_id: id,
    62→          account_handle: account_handle,
    63→          project_handle: project_handle
    64→        })
    65→
    66→        conn
    67→        |> put_resp_header("x-accel-redirect", local_path)
    68→        |> send_resp(:ok, "")
    69→
    70→      {:error, _} ->
    71→        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :download, :disk_miss], %{}, %{})
    72→
    73→        S3Transfers.enqueue_cas_download(account_handle, project_handle, key)
    74→
    75→        case S3.presign_download_url(key) do
    76→          {:ok, url} ->
    77→            conn
    78→            |> put_resp_header("x-accel-redirect", S3.remote_accel_path(url))
    79→            |> send_resp(:ok, "")
    80→
    81→          {:error, reason} ->
    82→            Sentry.capture_message("Failed to presign S3 url",
    83→              extra: %{
    84→                key: key,
    85→                reason: reason
    86→              }
    87→            )
    88→
    89→            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :download, :error], %{}, %{reason: inspect(reason)})
    90→
    91→            send_resp(conn, :not_found, "")
    92→        end
    93→    end
    94→  end
    95→
    96→  operation(:save,
    97→    summary: "Save a CAS artifact",
    98→    operation_id: "saveCASArtifact",
    99→    parameters: [
   100→      id: [
   101→        in: :path,
   102→        type: :string,
   103→        required: true,
   104→        description: "The artifact identifier"
   105→      ],
   106→      account_handle: [
   107→        in: :query,
   108→        type: :string,
   109→        required: true,
   110→        description: "The handle of the account"
   111→      ],
   112→      project_handle: [
   113→        in: :query,
   114→        type: :string,
   115→        required: true,
   116→        description: "The handle of the project"
   117→      ]
   118→    ],
   119→    request_body: {"The CAS artifact data", "application/octet-stream", nil, required: true},
   120→    responses: %{
   121→      no_content: {"Upload successful", nil, nil},
   122→      request_entity_too_large: {"Request body exceeded allowed size", "application/json", Error},
   123→      request_timeout: {"Request body read timed out", "application/json", Error},
   124→      internal_server_error: {"Failed to persist artifact", "application/json", Error},
   125→      unauthorized: {"Unauthorized", "application/json", Error},
   126→      forbidden: {"Forbidden", "application/json", Error},
   127→      bad_request: {"Bad request", "application/json", Error}
   128→    }
   129→  )
   130→
   131→  def save(conn, %{id: id, account_handle: account_handle, project_handle: project_handle}) do
   132→    if CAS.Disk.exists?(account_handle, project_handle, id) do
   133→      handle_existing_artifact(conn)
   134→    else
   135→      save_new_artifact(conn, account_handle, project_handle, id)
   136→    end
   137→  end
   138→
   139→  defp handle_existing_artifact(conn) do
   140→    :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :exists], %{count: 1}, %{})
   141→
   142→    case BodyReader.drain(conn) do
   143→      {:ok, conn_after} -> send_resp(conn_after, :no_content, "")
   144→      {:error, conn_after} -> send_resp(conn_after, :no_content, "")
   145→    end
   146→  end
   147→
   148→  defp save_new_artifact(conn, account_handle, project_handle, id) do
   149→    case CAS.Disk.ensure_artifact_directory(account_handle, project_handle, id) do
   150→      {:ok, target_dir} ->
   151→        case BodyReader.read(conn, tmp_dir: target_dir) do
   152→          {:ok, data, conn_after} ->
   153→            size = get_data_size(data)
   154→            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :attempt], %{size: size}, %{})
   155→            persist_artifact(conn_after, account_handle, project_handle, id, data, size)
   156→
   157→          {:error, :too_large, conn_after} ->
   158→            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :error], %{count: 1}, %{reason: :too_large})
   159→            send_error(conn_after, :request_entity_too_large, "Request body exceeded allowed size")
   160→
   161→          {:error, :timeout, conn_after} ->
   162→            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :error], %{count: 1}, %{reason: :timeout})
   163→            send_error(conn_after, :request_timeout, "Request body read timed out")
   164→
   165→          {:error, :cancelled, conn_after} ->
   166→            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :cancelled], %{count: 1}, %{})
   167→            send_resp(conn_after, :no_content, "")
   168→
   169→          {:error, _reason, conn_after} ->
   170→            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :error], %{count: 1}, %{reason: :read_error})
   171→            send_error(conn_after, :internal_server_error, "Failed to persist artifact")
   172→        end
   173→
   174→      {:error, reason} ->
   175→        Logger.error("Failed to ensure CAS artifact directory: #{inspect(reason)}")
   176→        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :error], %{count: 1}, %{reason: :persist_error})
   177→        send_error(conn, :internal_server_error, "Failed to persist artifact")
   178→    end
   179→  end
   180→
   181→  defp persist_artifact(conn, account_handle, project_handle, id, data, size) do
   182→    case CAS.Disk.put(account_handle, project_handle, id, data) do
   183→      :ok ->
   184→        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :success], %{size: size}, %{
   185→          cas_id: id,
   186→          account_handle: account_handle,
   187→          project_handle: project_handle
   188→        })
   189→
   190→        key = CAS.Disk.key(account_handle, project_handle, id)
   191→        :ok = CacheArtifacts.track_artifact_access(key)
   192→        S3Transfers.enqueue_cas_upload(account_handle, project_handle, key)
   193→        send_resp(conn, :no_content, "")
   194→
   195→      {:error, :exists} ->
   196→        cleanup_tmp_file(data)
   197→        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :exists], %{count: 1}, %{})
   198→        send_resp(conn, :no_content, "")
   199→
   200→      {:error, _reason} ->
   201→        cleanup_tmp_file(data)
   202→        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :error], %{count: 1}, %{reason: :persist_error})
   203→        send_error(conn, :internal_server_error, "Failed to persist artifact")
   204→    end
   205→  end
   206→
   207→  defp send_error(conn, status, message) do
   208→    conn
   209→    |> put_status(status)
   210→    |> json(%{message: message})
   211→  end
   212→
   213→  defp cleanup_tmp_file({:file, tmp_path}), do: File.rm(tmp_path)
   214→  defp cleanup_tmp_file(_binary_data), do: :ok
   215→
   216→  defp get_data_size({:file, tmp_path}) do
   217→    case File.stat(tmp_path) do
   218→      {:ok, %File.Stat{size: sz}} -> sz
   219→      _ -> 0
   220→    end
   221→  end
   222→
   223→  defp get_data_size(bin) when is_binary(bin), do: byte_size(bin)
   224→end
   225→

defmodule CacheWeb.CASController do
  use CacheWeb, :controller
  use OpenApiSpex.ControllerSpecs

  alias Cache.BodyReader
  alias Cache.CacheArtifacts
  alias Cache.CAS
  alias Cache.S3
  alias Cache.S3Transfers
  alias CacheWeb.API.Schemas.Error

  require Logger

  plug OpenApiSpex.Plug.CastAndValidate,
    json_render_error_v2: true

  tags(["CAS"])

  operation(:download,
    summary: "Download a CAS artifact",
    operation_id: "downloadCASArtifact",
    parameters: [
      id: [
        in: :path,
        type: :string,
        required: true,
        description: "The artifact identifier"
      ],
      account_handle: [
        in: :query,
        type: :string,
        required: true,
        description: "The handle of the account"
      ],
      project_handle: [
        in: :query,
        type: :string,
        required: true,
        description: "The handle of the project"
      ]
    ],
    responses: %{
      ok: {"Artifact content", "application/octet-stream", nil},
      not_found: {"Artifact not found", "application/json", Error},
      unauthorized: {"Unauthorized", "application/json", Error},
      forbidden: {"Forbidden", "application/json", Error},
      bad_request: {"Bad request", "application/json", Error}
    }
  )

  def download(conn, %{id: id, account_handle: account_handle, project_handle: project_handle}) do
    :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :download, :hit], %{}, %{})
    key = CAS.Disk.key(account_handle, project_handle, id)
    :ok = CacheArtifacts.track_artifact_access(key)

    case CAS.Disk.stat(account_handle, project_handle, id) do
      {:ok, %File.Stat{size: size}} ->
        local_path = CAS.Disk.local_accel_path(account_handle, project_handle, id)

        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :download, :disk_hit], %{size: size}, %{
          cas_id: id,
          account_handle: account_handle,
          project_handle: project_handle
        })

        conn
        |> put_resp_header("x-accel-redirect", local_path)
        |> send_resp(:ok, "")

      {:error, _} ->
        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :download, :disk_miss], %{}, %{})

        S3Transfers.enqueue_cas_download(account_handle, project_handle, key)

        case S3.presign_download_url(key) do
          {:ok, url} ->
            conn
            |> put_resp_header("x-accel-redirect", S3.remote_accel_path(url))
            |> send_resp(:ok, "")

          {:error, reason} ->
            Sentry.capture_message("Failed to presign S3 url",
              extra: %{
                key: key,
                reason: reason
              }
            )

            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :download, :error], %{}, %{reason: inspect(reason)})

            send_resp(conn, :not_found, "")
        end
    end
  end

  operation(:save,
    summary: "Save a CAS artifact",
    operation_id: "saveCASArtifact",
    parameters: [
      id: [
        in: :path,
        type: :string,
        required: true,
        description: "The artifact identifier"
      ],
      account_handle: [
        in: :query,
        type: :string,
        required: true,
        description: "The handle of the account"
      ],
      project_handle: [
        in: :query,
        type: :string,
        required: true,
        description: "The handle of the project"
      ]
    ],
    request_body: {"The CAS artifact data", "application/octet-stream", nil, required: true},
    responses: %{
      no_content: {"Upload successful", nil, nil},
      request_entity_too_large: {"Request body exceeded allowed size", "application/json", Error},
      request_timeout: {"Request body read timed out", "application/json", Error},
      internal_server_error: {"Failed to persist artifact", "application/json", Error},
      unauthorized: {"Unauthorized", "application/json", Error},
      forbidden: {"Forbidden", "application/json", Error},
      bad_request: {"Bad request", "application/json", Error}
    }
  )

  def save(conn, %{id: id, account_handle: account_handle, project_handle: project_handle}) do
    if CAS.Disk.exists?(account_handle, project_handle, id) do
      handle_existing_artifact(conn)
    else
      save_new_artifact(conn, account_handle, project_handle, id)
    end
  end

  defp handle_existing_artifact(conn) do
    :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :exists], %{count: 1}, %{})

    case BodyReader.drain(conn) do
      {:ok, conn_after} -> send_resp(conn_after, :no_content, "")
      {:error, conn_after} -> send_resp(conn_after, :no_content, "")
    end
  end

  defp save_new_artifact(conn, account_handle, project_handle, id) do
    case CAS.Disk.ensure_artifact_directory(account_handle, project_handle, id) do
      {:ok, target_dir} ->
        case BodyReader.read(conn, tmp_dir: target_dir) do
          {:ok, data, conn_after} ->
            size = get_data_size(data)
            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :attempt], %{size: size}, %{})
            persist_artifact(conn_after, account_handle, project_handle, id, data, size)

          {:error, :too_large, conn_after} ->
            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :error], %{count: 1}, %{reason: :too_large})
            send_error(conn_after, :request_entity_too_large, "Request body exceeded allowed size")

          {:error, :timeout, conn_after} ->
            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :error], %{count: 1}, %{reason: :timeout})
            send_error(conn_after, :request_timeout, "Request body read timed out")

          {:error, :cancelled, conn_after} ->
            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :cancelled], %{count: 1}, %{})
            send_resp(conn_after, :no_content, "")

          {:error, _reason, conn_after} ->
            :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :error], %{count: 1}, %{reason: :read_error})
            send_error(conn_after, :internal_server_error, "Failed to persist artifact")
        end

      {:error, reason} ->
        Logger.error("Failed to ensure CAS artifact directory: #{inspect(reason)}")
        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :error], %{count: 1}, %{reason: :persist_error})
        send_error(conn, :internal_server_error, "Failed to persist artifact")
    end
  end

  defp persist_artifact(conn, account_handle, project_handle, id, data, size) do
    case CAS.Disk.put(account_handle, project_handle, id, data) do
      :ok ->
        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :success], %{size: size}, %{
          cas_id: id,
          account_handle: account_handle,
          project_handle: project_handle
        })

        key = CAS.Disk.key(account_handle, project_handle, id)
        :ok = CacheArtifacts.track_artifact_access(key)
        S3Transfers.enqueue_cas_upload(account_handle, project_handle, key)
        send_resp(conn, :no_content, "")

      {:error, :exists} ->
        cleanup_tmp_file(data)
        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :exists], %{count: 1}, %{})
        send_resp(conn, :no_content, "")

      {:error, _reason} ->
        cleanup_tmp_file(data)
        :telemetry.execute([:cache, :cas, :upload, :error], %{count: 1}, %{reason: :persist_error})
        send_error(conn, :internal_server_error, "Failed to persist artifact")
    end
  end

  defp send_error(conn, status, message) do
    conn
    |> put_status(status)
    |> json(%{message: message})
  end

  defp cleanup_tmp_file({:file, tmp_path}), do: File.rm(tmp_path)
  defp cleanup_tmp_file(_binary_data), do: :ok

  defp get_data_size({:file, tmp_path}) do
    case File.stat(tmp_path) do
      {:ok, %File.Stat{size: sz}} -> sz
      _ -> 0
    end
  end

  defp get_data_size(bin) when is_binary(bin), do: byte_size(bin)
end

content.text:
I see the issue. The `drain/1` function in `Cache.BodyReader` (line 103-110) calls `Plug.Conn.read_body` but doesn't rescue `Bandit.HTTPError` -- it only rescues `Bandit.TransportError` in the `read` and `read_loop` functions. When the client disconnects during `drain`, Bandit raises `HTTPError: Body read timeout` which bubbles up unhandled, and Sentry captures it.

The fix has two parts:
1. The Sentry filter change (already done) stops reporting these to Sentry
2. The `drain` function should also rescue `Bandit.HTTPError` to handle it gracefully

Let me fix the `drain` function too.

      {:ok, _, conn_after} -> {:ok, conn_after}
      {:error, _reason, conn_after} -> {:error, conn_after}
    end
  end

defmodule Cache.BodyReader do
  @moduledoc """
  Handles reading request bodies from Plug connections.

  Small bodies are read into memory, large bodies are streamed to temporary files
  to avoid memory pressure.

  ## Timeout Strategy

  Uses progressive timeouts inspired by Apache's `mod_reqtimeout` MinRate:
  - Initial timeout is calculated based on Content-Length and minimum throughput
  - Each subsequent chunk read uses a fresh timeout based on chunk size
  - This allows slow-but-steady connections while timing out stalled transfers

  See `TuistCommon.BodyReader` for the timeout calculation logic.
  """

  @max_upload_bytes 25 * 1024 * 1024
  @default_opts [length: @max_upload_bytes, read_length: 262_144]

  @doc """
  Reads the request body from the connection.

  Returns:
  - `{:ok, body, conn}` - For small bodies that fit in memory
  - `{:ok, {:file, tmp_path}, conn}` - For large bodies streamed to temp file
  - `{:error, reason, conn}` - For errors like :too_large, :timeout, etc.
  """

  def read(conn, opts \\ []) do
    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(read_opts(conn), opts)
    max_bytes = Keyword.get(opts, :max_bytes, @max_upload_bytes)

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
    |> handle_read_result(conn, merged_opts, :store, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  @doc """
  Reads the request body and writes it directly to an IO device.

  Returns `{:ok, bytes_written, conn}` on success,
  or `{:error, reason, conn}` on failure.
  """
  def read_to_device(conn, device, opts \\ []) do
    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(read_opts(conn), opts)
    max_bytes = Keyword.get(opts, :max_bytes, @max_upload_bytes)
    writer = fn chunk -> :file.write(device, chunk) end

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
    |> handle_device_result(conn, merged_opts, writer, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:ok, body, conn_after}, _conn, _opts, writer, max_bytes) do
    bytes = byte_size(body)

    cond do
      bytes > max_bytes -> {:error, :too_large, conn_after}
      bytes == 0 -> {:ok, 0, conn_after}
      true -> write_device_body(writer, body, bytes, conn_after)
    end
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:more, chunk, conn_after}, _conn, opts, writer, max_bytes) do
    bytes_read = byte_size(chunk)

    if bytes_read > max_bytes do
      {:error, :too_large, conn_after}
    else
      with :ok <- writer.(chunk),
           {:ok, conn_final, total_bytes} <- read_loop(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
        {:ok, total_bytes, conn_final}
      else
        {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason, conn_after}
        {:error, reason, conn_final} -> {:error, reason, conn_final}
      end
    end
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:error, reason}, conn, _opts, _writer, _max_bytes) do
    normalize_error(reason, conn)
  end

  defp write_device_body(writer, body, bytes, conn_after) do
    case writer.(body) do
      :ok -> {:ok, bytes, conn_after}
      {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason, conn_after}
    end
  end

  @doc """
  Drains the request body without storing it.
  Useful when the upload already exists and we need to consume the body.
  """

  def drain(conn) do
    opts = read_opts(conn)

    case conn |> Plug.Conn.read_body(opts) |> handle_read_result(conn, opts, :discard, @max_upload_bytes) do
      {:ok, _, conn_after} -> {:ok, conn_after}
      {:error, _reason, conn_after} -> {:error, conn_after}
    end
  end

  defp handle_read_result(result, conn, opts, mode, max_bytes) do
    case result do
      {:ok, body, conn_after} ->
        {:ok, body, conn_after}

      {:more, chunk, conn_after} ->
        read_chunks(conn_after, opts, chunk, byte_size(chunk), mode, max_bytes)

      {:error, :too_large} ->
        {:error, :too_large, conn}

      {:error, reason} when reason in [:timeout, :econnaborted] ->
        {:error, :timeout, conn}

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp normalize_error(:too_large, conn), do: {:error, :too_large, conn}
  defp normalize_error(reason, conn) when reason in [:timeout, :econnaborted], do: {:error, :timeout, conn}
  defp normalize_error(reason, conn), do: {:error, reason, conn}

  defp read_chunks(conn, opts, _first_chunk, bytes_read, :discard, max_bytes) do
    read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, fn _chunk -> :ok end, max_bytes)
  end

  defp read_chunks(conn, opts, first_chunk, bytes_read, :store, max_bytes) do
    tmp_path = tmp_path(opts)

    case File.open(tmp_path, [:write, :binary]) do
      {:ok, device} ->
        writer = fn chunk -> :file.write(device, chunk) end

        case writer.(first_chunk) do
          :ok ->
            case read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
              {:ok, conn_after, _bytes} ->
                File.close(device)
                {:ok, {:file, tmp_path}, conn_after}

              {:error, reason, conn_after} ->
                File.close(device)
                File.rm(tmp_path)
                {:error, reason, conn_after}
            end

          {:error, reason} ->
            File.close(device)
            File.rm(tmp_path)
            {:error, reason, conn}
        end

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
    chunk_opts = Keyword.put(opts, :read_timeout, chunk_timeout(opts))

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(chunk_opts)
    |> handle_loop_result(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  defp handle_loop_result(result, conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
    case result do
      {:ok, "", conn_after} ->
        {:ok, conn_after, bytes_read}

      {:ok, chunk, conn_after} ->
        write_chunk(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, false)

      {:more, chunk, conn_after} ->
        write_chunk(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, true)

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp write_chunk(conn, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, recurse?) do
    bytes = bytes_read + byte_size(chunk)

    if bytes > max_bytes do
      {:error, :too_large, conn}
    else
      case writer.(chunk) do
        :ok ->
          if recurse? do
            read_loop(conn, opts, bytes, writer, max_bytes)
          else
            {:ok, conn, bytes}
          end

        {:error, reason} ->
          {:error, reason, conn}
      end
    end
  end

  defp tmp_path(opts) do
    base = Keyword.get(opts, :tmp_dir, System.tmp_dir!())
    unique = :erlang.unique_integer([:positive, :monotonic])
    Path.join(base, ".cache-upload-#{unique}")
  end

  defp read_opts(conn) do
    base_opts = Map.get(conn.private, :body_read_opts, @default_opts)
    TuistCommon.BodyReader.read_opts(conn, base_opts)
  end

  defp chunk_timeout(opts) do
    TuistCommon.BodyReader.chunk_timeout(opts)
  end
end

defmodule Cache.BodyReader do
  @moduledoc """
  Handles reading request bodies from Plug connections.

  Small bodies are read into memory, large bodies are streamed to temporary files
  to avoid memory pressure.

  ## Timeout Strategy

  Uses progressive timeouts inspired by Apache's `mod_reqtimeout` MinRate:
  - Initial timeout is calculated based on Content-Length and minimum throughput
  - Each subsequent chunk read uses a fresh timeout based on chunk size
  - This allows slow-but-steady connections while timing out stalled transfers

  See `TuistCommon.BodyReader` for the timeout calculation logic.
  """

  @max_upload_bytes 25 * 1024 * 1024
  @default_opts [length: @max_upload_bytes, read_length: 262_144]

  @doc """
  Reads the request body from the connection.

  Returns:
  - `{:ok, body, conn}` - For small bodies that fit in memory
  - `{:ok, {:file, tmp_path}, conn}` - For large bodies streamed to temp file
  - `{:error, reason, conn}` - For errors like :too_large, :timeout, etc.
  """

  def read(conn, opts \\ []) do
    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(read_opts(conn), opts)
    max_bytes = Keyword.get(opts, :max_bytes, @max_upload_bytes)

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
    |> handle_read_result(conn, merged_opts, :store, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  @doc """
  Reads the request body and writes it directly to an IO device.

  Returns `{:ok, bytes_written, conn}` on success,
  or `{:error, reason, conn}` on failure.
  """
  def read_to_device(conn, device, opts \\ []) do
    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(read_opts(conn), opts)
    max_bytes = Keyword.get(opts, :max_bytes, @max_upload_bytes)
    writer = fn chunk -> :file.write(device, chunk) end

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
    |> handle_device_result(conn, merged_opts, writer, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:ok, body, conn_after}, _conn, _opts, writer, max_bytes) do
    bytes = byte_size(body)

    cond do
      bytes > max_bytes -> {:error, :too_large, conn_after}
      bytes == 0 -> {:ok, 0, conn_after}
      true -> write_device_body(writer, body, bytes, conn_after)
    end
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:more, chunk, conn_after}, _conn, opts, writer, max_bytes) do
    bytes_read = byte_size(chunk)

    if bytes_read > max_bytes do
      {:error, :too_large, conn_after}
    else
      with :ok <- writer.(chunk),
           {:ok, conn_final, total_bytes} <- read_loop(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
        {:ok, total_bytes, conn_final}
      else
        {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason, conn_after}
        {:error, reason, conn_final} -> {:error, reason, conn_final}
      end
    end
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:error, reason}, conn, _opts, _writer, _max_bytes) do
    normalize_error(reason, conn)
  end

  defp write_device_body(writer, body, bytes, conn_after) do
    case writer.(body) do
      :ok -> {:ok, bytes, conn_after}
      {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason, conn_after}
    end
  end

  @doc """
  Drains the request body without storing it.
  Useful when the upload already exists and we need to consume the body.
  """

  def drain(conn) do
    opts = read_opts(conn)

    case conn |> Plug.Conn.read_body(opts) |> handle_read_result(conn, opts, :discard, @max_upload_bytes) do
      {:ok, _, conn_after} -> {:ok, conn_after}
      {:error, _reason, conn_after} -> {:error, conn_after}
    end
  rescue
    Bandit.HTTPError -> {:error, conn}
    Bandit.TransportError -> {:error, conn}
  end

  defp handle_read_result(result, conn, opts, mode, max_bytes) do
    case result do
      {:ok, body, conn_after} ->
        {:ok, body, conn_after}

      {:more, chunk, conn_after} ->
        read_chunks(conn_after, opts, chunk, byte_size(chunk), mode, max_bytes)

      {:error, :too_large} ->
        {:error, :too_large, conn}

      {:error, reason} when reason in [:timeout, :econnaborted] ->
        {:error, :timeout, conn}

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp normalize_error(:too_large, conn), do: {:error, :too_large, conn}
  defp normalize_error(reason, conn) when reason in [:timeout, :econnaborted], do: {:error, :timeout, conn}
  defp normalize_error(reason, conn), do: {:error, reason, conn}

  defp read_chunks(conn, opts, _first_chunk, bytes_read, :discard, max_bytes) do
    read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, fn _chunk -> :ok end, max_bytes)
  end

  defp read_chunks(conn, opts, first_chunk, bytes_read, :store, max_bytes) do
    tmp_path = tmp_path(opts)

    case File.open(tmp_path, [:write, :binary]) do
      {:ok, device} ->
        writer = fn chunk -> :file.write(device, chunk) end

        case writer.(first_chunk) do
          :ok ->
            case read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
              {:ok, conn_after, _bytes} ->
                File.close(device)
                {:ok, {:file, tmp_path}, conn_after}

              {:error, reason, conn_after} ->
                File.close(device)
                File.rm(tmp_path)
                {:error, reason, conn_after}
            end

          {:error, reason} ->
            File.close(device)
            File.rm(tmp_path)
            {:error, reason, conn}
        end

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
    chunk_opts = Keyword.put(opts, :read_timeout, chunk_timeout(opts))

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(chunk_opts)
    |> handle_loop_result(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  defp handle_loop_result(result, conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
    case result do
      {:ok, "", conn_after} ->
        {:ok, conn_after, bytes_read}

      {:ok, chunk, conn_after} ->
        write_chunk(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, false)

      {:more, chunk, conn_after} ->
        write_chunk(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, true)

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

    bytes = bytes_read + byte_size(chunk)

    if bytes > max_bytes do
      {:error, :too_large, conn}

      case writer.(chunk) do
        :ok ->
          if recurse? do
            read_loop(conn, opts, bytes, writer, max_bytes)
          else
            {:ok, conn, bytes}
          end

        {:error, reason} ->
          {:error, reason, conn}
      end
    end
  end

  defp tmp_path(opts) do
    base = Keyword.get(opts, :tmp_dir, System.tmp_dir!())
    unique = :erlang.unique_integer([:positive, :monotonic])
    Path.join(base, ".cache-upload-#{unique}")
  end

  defp read_opts(conn) do
    base_opts = Map.get(conn.private, :body_read_opts, @default_opts)
    TuistCommon.BodyReader.read_opts(conn, base_opts)
  end

  defp chunk_timeout(opts) do
    TuistCommon.BodyReader.chunk_timeout(opts)
  end
end

defmodule Cache.BodyReader do
  @moduledoc """
  Handles reading request bodies from Plug connections.

  Small bodies are read into memory, large bodies are streamed to temporary files
  to avoid memory pressure.

  ## Timeout Strategy

  Uses progressive timeouts inspired by Apache's `mod_reqtimeout` MinRate:
  - Initial timeout is calculated based on Content-Length and minimum throughput
  - Each subsequent chunk read uses a fresh timeout based on chunk size
  - This allows slow-but-steady connections while timing out stalled transfers

  See `TuistCommon.BodyReader` for the timeout calculation logic.
  """

  @max_upload_bytes 25 * 1024 * 1024
  @default_opts [length: @max_upload_bytes, read_length: 262_144]

  @doc """
  Reads the request body from the connection.

  Returns:
  - `{:ok, body, conn}` - For small bodies that fit in memory
  - `{:ok, {:file, tmp_path}, conn}` - For large bodies streamed to temp file
  - `{:error, reason, conn}` - For errors like :too_large, :timeout, etc.
  """

  def read(conn, opts \\ []) do
    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(read_opts(conn), opts)
    max_bytes = Keyword.get(opts, :max_bytes, @max_upload_bytes)

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
    |> handle_read_result(conn, merged_opts, :store, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.HTTPError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}

    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  @doc """
  Reads the request body and writes it directly to an IO device.

  Returns `{:ok, bytes_written, conn}` on success,
  or `{:error, reason, conn}` on failure.
  """
  def read_to_device(conn, device, opts \\ []) do
    merged_opts = Keyword.merge(read_opts(conn), opts)
    max_bytes = Keyword.get(opts, :max_bytes, @max_upload_bytes)
    writer = fn chunk -> :file.write(device, chunk) end

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
    |> handle_device_result(conn, merged_opts, writer, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:ok, body, conn_after}, _conn, _opts, writer, max_bytes) do
    bytes = byte_size(body)

    cond do
      bytes > max_bytes -> {:error, :too_large, conn_after}
      bytes == 0 -> {:ok, 0, conn_after}
      true -> write_device_body(writer, body, bytes, conn_after)
    end
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:more, chunk, conn_after}, _conn, opts, writer, max_bytes) do
    bytes_read = byte_size(chunk)

    if bytes_read > max_bytes do
      {:error, :too_large, conn_after}
    else
      with :ok <- writer.(chunk),
           {:ok, conn_final, total_bytes} <- read_loop(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
        {:ok, total_bytes, conn_final}
      else
        {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason, conn_after}
        {:error, reason, conn_final} -> {:error, reason, conn_final}
      end
    end
  end

  defp handle_device_result({:error, reason}, conn, _opts, _writer, _max_bytes) do
    normalize_error(reason, conn)
  end

  defp write_device_body(writer, body, bytes, conn_after) do
    case writer.(body) do
      :ok -> {:ok, bytes, conn_after}
      {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason, conn_after}
    end
  end

  @doc """
  Drains the request body without storing it.
  Useful when the upload already exists and we need to consume the body.
  """

  def drain(conn) do
    opts = read_opts(conn)

    case conn |> Plug.Conn.read_body(opts) |> handle_read_result(conn, opts, :discard, @max_upload_bytes) do
      {:ok, _, conn_after} -> {:ok, conn_after}
      {:error, _reason, conn_after} -> {:error, conn_after}
    end
  rescue
    Bandit.HTTPError -> {:error, conn}
    Bandit.TransportError -> {:error, conn}
  end

  defp handle_read_result(result, conn, opts, mode, max_bytes) do
    case result do
      {:ok, body, conn_after} ->
        {:ok, body, conn_after}

      {:more, chunk, conn_after} ->
        read_chunks(conn_after, opts, chunk, byte_size(chunk), mode, max_bytes)

      {:error, :too_large} ->
        {:error, :too_large, conn}

      {:error, reason} when reason in [:timeout, :econnaborted] ->
        {:error, :timeout, conn}

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp normalize_error(:too_large, conn), do: {:error, :too_large, conn}
  defp normalize_error(reason, conn) when reason in [:timeout, :econnaborted], do: {:error, :timeout, conn}
  defp normalize_error(reason, conn), do: {:error, reason, conn}

  defp read_chunks(conn, opts, _first_chunk, bytes_read, :discard, max_bytes) do
    read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, fn _chunk -> :ok end, max_bytes)
  end

  defp read_chunks(conn, opts, first_chunk, bytes_read, :store, max_bytes) do
    tmp_path = tmp_path(opts)

    case File.open(tmp_path, [:write, :binary]) do
      {:ok, device} ->
        writer = fn chunk -> :file.write(device, chunk) end

        case writer.(first_chunk) do
          :ok ->
            case read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
              {:ok, conn_after, _bytes} ->
                File.close(device)
                {:ok, {:file, tmp_path}, conn_after}

              {:error, reason, conn_after} ->
                File.close(device)
                File.rm(tmp_path)
                {:error, reason, conn_after}
            end

          {:error, reason} ->
            File.close(device)
            File.rm(tmp_path)
            {:error, reason, conn}
        end

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp read_loop(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
    chunk_opts = Keyword.put(opts, :read_timeout, chunk_timeout(opts))

    conn
    |> Plug.Conn.read_body(chunk_opts)
    |> handle_loop_result(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes)
  rescue
    Bandit.TransportError ->
      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
  end

  defp handle_loop_result(result, conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes) do
    case result do
      {:ok, "", conn_after} ->
        {:ok, conn_after, bytes_read}

      {:ok, chunk, conn_after} ->
        write_chunk(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, false)

      {:more, chunk, conn_after} ->
        write_chunk(conn_after, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, true)

      {:error, reason} ->
        {:error, reason, conn}
    end
  end

  defp write_chunk(conn, opts, bytes_read, chunk, writer, max_bytes, recurse?) do
    bytes = bytes_read + byte_size(chunk)

    if bytes > max_bytes do
      {:error, :too_large, conn}
    else
      case writer.(chunk) do
        :ok ->
          if recurse? do
            read_loop(conn, opts, bytes, writer, max_bytes)
          else
            {:ok, conn, bytes}
          end

        {:error, reason} ->
          {:error, reason, conn}
      end
    end
  end

  defp tmp_path(opts) do
    base = Keyword.get(opts, :tmp_dir, System.tmp_dir!())
    unique = :erlang.unique_integer([:positive, :monotonic])
    Path.join(base, ".cache-upload-#{unique}")
  end

  defp read_opts(conn) do
    base_opts = Map.get(conn.private, :body_read_opts, @default_opts)
    TuistCommon.BodyReader.read_opts(conn, base_opts)
  end

  defp chunk_timeout(opts) do
    TuistCommon.BodyReader.chunk_timeout(opts)
  end
end

content.text:
The server's webhook plug already catches `Bandit.HTTPError` but only for `:request_timeout` and re-raises others. Looking at the Sentry trace, the error *is* a body read timeout, so the `plug_status` check should match. But wait -- the Sentry stacktrace shows it's being raised from Bandit's pipeline, not from within the `try` block. Let me check if the error is happening at a different layer.

Looking at the server stacktrace again:
```
at Bandit.Pipeline.run/5 (lib/bandit/pipeline.ex:46)
at anonymous fn/1 in Bandit.Pipeline.commit_response!/1 (lib/bandit/pipeline.ex:192)
at Bandit.HTTPTransport.Bandit.HTTP1.Socket.request_error!/2 (lib/bandit/http1/socket.ex:494)
```

This is raised at the Bandit pipeline level, outside the plug chain -- the `try/rescue` in the webhook plug can't catch it. That's why the Sentry filter is the right fix for the server side.

diff --git a/cache/lib/cache/body_reader.ex b/cache/lib/cache/body_reader.ex
index 9a0678303b..62cfd5b00e 100644
--- a/cache/lib/cache/body_reader.ex
+++ b/cache/lib/cache/body_reader.ex
@@ -35,6 +35,9 @@ defmodule Cache.BodyReader do
     |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
     |> handle_read_result(conn, merged_opts, :store, max_bytes)
   rescue
+    Bandit.HTTPError ->
+      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
+
     Bandit.TransportError ->
       {:error, :cancelled, conn}
   end
@@ -54,6 +57,9 @@ defmodule Cache.BodyReader do
     |> Plug.Conn.read_body(merged_opts)
     |> handle_device_result(conn, merged_opts, writer, max_bytes)
   rescue
+    Bandit.HTTPError ->
+      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
+
     Bandit.TransportError ->
       {:error, :cancelled, conn}
   end
@@ -107,6 +113,9 @@ defmodule Cache.BodyReader do
       {:ok, _, conn_after} -> {:ok, conn_after}
       {:error, _reason, conn_after} -> {:error, conn_after}
     end
+  rescue
+    Bandit.HTTPError -> {:error, conn}
+    Bandit.TransportError -> {:error, conn}
   end
 
   defp handle_read_result(result, conn, opts, mode, max_bytes) do
@@ -174,6 +183,9 @@ defmodule Cache.BodyReader do
     |> Plug.Conn.read_body(chunk_opts)
     |> handle_loop_result(conn, opts, bytes_read, writer, max_bytes)
   rescue
+    Bandit.HTTPError ->
+      {:error, :cancelled, conn}
+
     Bandit.TransportError ->
       {:error, :cancelled, conn}
   end

diff --git a/tuist_common/test/tuist_common/sentry_event_filter_test.exs b/tuist_common/test/tuist_common/sentry_event_filter_test.exs
index 7d5daa0d8a..b85d56f0f9 100644
--- a/tuist_common/test/tuist_common/sentry_event_filter_test.exs
+++ b/tuist_common/test/tuist_common/sentry_event_filter_test.exs
@@ -13,6 +13,11 @@ defmodule TuistCommon.SentryEventFilterTest do
   end
 
   describe "before_send/1" do
+    test "excludes Bandit.HTTPError" do
+      event = event(%{original_exception: %Bandit.HTTPError{message: "Body read timeout"}})
+      assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == false
+    end
+
     test "excludes Bandit.TransportError" do
       event = event(%{original_exception: %Bandit.TransportError{message: "test"}})
       assert SentryEventFilter.before_send(event) == false

input.command:
git checkout -b pedro/ignore-bandit-http-error-sentry && git add cache/lib/cache/body_reader.ex tuist_common/lib/tuist_common/sentry_event_filter.ex tuist_common/test/tuist_common/sentry_event_filter_test.exs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(server): ignore Bandit.HTTPError in Sentry and handle it in cache body reader

Bandit.HTTPError (body read timeout) fires frequently when clients disconnect
mid-request. This adds it to the shared Sentry filter to stop consuming quota,
and rescues it in the cache BodyReader alongside the existing TransportError
handling.
EOF
)"

input.command:
git push -u origin pedro/ignore-bandit-http-error-sentry && mise exec gh -- gh pr create --title "fix(server): ignore Bandit.HTTPError in Sentry and handle it in cache body reader" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Adds `Bandit.HTTPError` to the shared Sentry event filter so body read timeouts stop consuming our Sentry quota (~55K events across server and cache)
- Rescues `Bandit.HTTPError` in the cache `BodyReader` functions (`read`, `read_to_device`, `drain`, `read_loop`) alongside the existing `Bandit.TransportError` handling, so client disconnects during uploads are handled gracefully instead of bubbling up as unhandled exceptions

## Test plan

- [x] `tuist_common` Sentry filter tests pass (9 tests, including new `Bandit.HTTPError` test)
- [x] `cache` body reader tests pass
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/tuist/tuist/pull/9481

Here's what the PR does:

1. **Shared Sentry filter** (`tuist_common`): Added `Bandit.HTTPError` to the default ignored exceptions list. This stops both TUIST-7 (~29K events) and CACHE-7 (~25K events) from being reported to Sentry.

2. **Cache body reader**: Added `rescue Bandit.HTTPError` alongside the existing `Bandit.TransportError` rescues in all four functions (`read`, `read_to_device`, `drain`, `read_loop`). This means the cache now handles body read timeouts gracefully as `:cancelled` instead of letting them bubble up as unhandled exceptions.

The server-side webhook error (TUIST-7) is raised at the Bandit pipeline level outside the plug chain, so it can only be filtered at the Sentry level -- which the shared filter now handles.
